---
title: "Client Report - What's in a Name?"
subtitle: "w04 Elliot Stretch Activity"
author: "Ella Patten"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-copy: hover
    code-tools:
        source: false
        toggle: true
        caption: See code
execute: 
  warning: false
    
---

```{python}
import polars as pl
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
from lets_plot import *

LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)
```

```{python}
# Learn morea about Code Cells: https://quarto.org/docs/reference/cells/cells-jupyter.html

# Include and execute your code here
df = pl.read_csv("https://posit.byui.edu/content/0d43d66f-d3b7-4c43-8c36-52e3ed69e7f1/names_year_csv.csv")
```

```{python}
# Reproduce the chart Elliot using the data from the names_year.csv file. If, after a couple of hours, you can get the chart close, but not exactly the same, that is okay

elliot = df.filter(pl.col("name") == "Elliot").with_columns(
    pl.lit("Elliot").alias("name")
)

label_df = pd.DataFrame({
    "x": [1981, 1986, 2003],
    "y": [3000, 3000, 2900],
    "label": ["E.T Released", "Second release", "Third release"],
    "hjust": [1, 0, 0]
})

(ggplot(elliot, aes(x="year", y="Total"))
+ geom_line(aes(color="name"))
+ scale_color_manual(values={"Elliot": "blue"})
+ geom_vline(xintercept=1982, linetype="dashed", color="red")
+ geom_vline(xintercept=1985, linetype="dashed", color="red")
+ geom_vline(xintercept=2002, linetype="dashed", color="red")
+ geom_text(aes(x="x", y="y", label="label", hjust="hjust"), data=label_df, color="gray")
+ labs(title = "Usage of the name 'Elliot' vs releases of the movie 'E.T'")
+ scale_x_continuous(format="{d}")
+ theme(panel_background=element_rect(fill="lightgray"))
)

```

```{python}
import polars as pl
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import requests, io
from lets_plot import *
LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)

url = "https://github.com/byuidatascience/data4missing/raw/master/data-raw/flights_missing/flights_missing.json"
response = requests.get(url)
df = pl.read_json(io.BytesIO(response.content))

# Count rows where month == "n/a"
print(df.select(pl.all().is_null().sum()))

```
